item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia faces worker shortage crisis as war depletes labor force
item.description: Russia has run out of workers for the first time in its post-Soviet history, with record-low 2.1% unemployment driven by military conscription, emigration, and demographics. Central Bank warns labor shortages are driving inflation, forcing interest rates to 14.5%. [Spectator Australia]

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian drone strikes on Tuapse refinery cause major oil spill disaster
item.description: Hundreds of tons of oil leaked into the Black Sea after Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia's Tuapse refinery, creating an ecological disaster. Russian authorities delayed declaring emergency for 12 days, worsening contamination of air, soil and water. [Kyiv Independent]

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia and UAE lead push for Hormuz-bypass trade corridors
item.description: Middle Eastern states are dusting off proposals for overland pipelines and rail-sea corridors to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, building a new trade architecture connecting Indian Ocean ports to Mediterranean via Saudi Arabia, Jordan and Syria. [SCMP]

item.title: 🇻🇦 Rubio to Visit Vatican and Italy After Trump-Pope Tensions
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to the Vatican and Italy for meetings this week, following President Trump's public attacks on Pope Leo that drew criticism from Christians across the political spectrum. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Pays Steep Price as Israel Dismantles Its Strongholds
item.description: Israel has displaced hundreds of thousands of Hezbollah's Shi'ite constituents and killed thousands of its fighters, while domestic rivals harden opposition against the group's armed status in Lebanon. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇸🇰🇷🇺 Slovakia PM Fico to Attend Victory Day Parade in Moscow
item.description: Prime Minister Robert Fico is among a handful of global leaders attending Russia's scaled-back Victory Day parade, highlighting divisions within Europe over engagement with Moscow. [Reuters]

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ Agrees Third Oil Output Hike Since Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ will agree to a modest oil output hike, but the increase will remain largely symbolic as the US-Iran war continues to disrupt Gulf oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea Rejects US Cyber Threat Claims as Fabrication
item.description: Pyongyang's foreign ministry dismissed US accusations of cyber threats as disinformation aimed at justifying Washington's hostile policy, warning of countermeasures. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President Defies China with Surprise Eswatini Visit
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te arrived in Eswatini for a surprise diplomatic visit, asserting Taiwan right to engage with the world despite Beijing calling him a rat and attempting to stop the trip. [Reuters]

item.title: 🚢 Two Dead as Migrant Boat Runs Aground in France
item.description: A small boat carrying 82 migrants ran aground on a beach in northern France, leaving two women dead and 16 injured. This is the third deadly incident involving Channel crossings in just over a month. [AP News]

item.title: 🇮🇱✈️ Israel Approves Purchase of F-35 and F-15IA Fighter Jet Squadrons
item.description: Israel gave final approval to purchase two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA fighter aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing, part of a $119 billion military buildup plan for the coming decade. [Straits Times]

item.title: 🛢️🚨 Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Yemen Coast Near Somalia
item.description: An oil tanker was hijacked off the coast of Yemen and taken towards Somalia, marking the second such incident in the past 10 days amid ongoing regional instability. [BBC]

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 Cuba Condemns New US Sanctions as Illegal and Abusive
item.description: Cuba denounced new US sanctions as illegal and abusive, adding to existing US oil blockade measures that have caused widespread blackouts and fuel shortages across the island nation. [BBC]

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 NATO seeks answers from US on Germany troop cuts
item.description: NATO says it is working with the US to understand details of planned troop reductions in Germany, after Trump announced deeper cuts beyond the initial 5,000 withdrawal. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine strikes Russian Baltic port with drone swarm
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit Primorsk oil terminal near St Petersburg, marking a major escalation as Ukraine extends its reach deep into Russian territory. The port handles significant oil exports. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Mittelstand shows resilience amid industrial decline
item.description: Small and midsize enterprises (SMEs) remain the backbone of Germanys economy, with hidden champions like Zeiss and Herrenknecht maintaining global leadership in specialized sectors despite broader industrial struggles. [Deutsche Welle]

item.title: 🇲🇲 Myanmar military gains ground but civil war far from over
item.description: The junta has retaken territory and opened political channels after rigged elections installed Min Aung Hlaing as president, but resistance groups still control significant areas and armed opposition persists. [Philippine Daily Inquirer]

item.title: 🇹🇭 Bangkok street food culture under threat from vendor crackdown
item.description: Thailands capital is relocating street vendors from busy districts, raising concerns about livelihoods and the iconic food culture that defines Bangkok. [SCMP]

item.title: 🇳🇿🇸🇬 New Zealand PM Luxon visits Singapore for annual leaders meeting
item.description: Christopher Luxons three-day visit focuses on strengthening the New Zealand-Singapore Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, with agreements on trade in essential supplies. [Channel News Asia]

item.title: 🛢️ Americas energy dominance strategy faces long-term risks
item.description: A Straits Times analysis argues that Trumps approach to energy geopolitics - from sanctions to military action in Iran - may undermine US oil industry competitiveness as other nations accelerate clean energy adoption. [Straits Times]

item.title: 🇨🇳🇮🇷 China issues injunction blocking US sanctions on five refineries
item.description: Chinas Commerce Ministry moved to block US sanctions on five Chinese refiners accused of buying Iranian oil, issuing an injunction through state news agency Xinhua as Beijing pushes back against Washington pressure campaign. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 Senior Republicans break with Trump over Germany troop withdrawal
item.description: Two top Republican lawmakers expressed concern about the Pentagon decision to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, highlighting bipartisan unease over the timing amid Iran war tensions and tariff disputes straining transatlantic relations. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇰🇵🇷🇺 North Korea mobilizes youth for war effort alongside Russia
item.description: Kim Jong Un addressed the ruling party youth congress in Pyongyang, emphasizing young people central to domestic mobilization and North Korea military role in Russia war against Ukraine, as the two countries discuss long-term military cooperation through 2031. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russia and Ukraine give conflicting accounts of Sumy village capture
item.description: Russia Defense Ministry claimed troops seized Myropillia village in Ukraines Sumy region, but Ukrainian military denied the village changed hands, as fighting continues along multiple fronts in the east. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Military Orders Evacuation of 11 Southern Lebanon Towns
item.description: The Israeli military issued an urgent evacuation order for residents of 11 towns and villages in southern Lebanon, telling them to move at least 1,000 meters away from their homes amid ongoing operations against Hezbollah. [Reuters]
